title: Sticky Gochujang & Roasted Aubergine Ragù with Rice
summary: This speedy dinner packs serious punch. Smoky roasted aubergine folded through your Sausage Gochujang Ragù batch sauce, served with fluffy rice and topped with crispy chilli and ginger.

method.description: Before you begin, make the Sausage Gochujang Ragú batch recipe.
Heat the oven to 200°C. Chop your aubergine into large chunks and toss with olive oil, salt and black pepper. Spread out on a tray and roast for 25–30 mins until golden and soft.
Meanwhile, cook the rice following pack instructions. Fluff with a fork and set aside.
Finely slice the chilli and cut the ginger into thin matchsticks.
Warm the Sausage Gochujang Ragù in a saucepan over a low heat. Once the aubergine is ready, stir it through the ragù and keep warm.
In a small frying pan, heat the sesame oil over a low heat. Add the ginger, chilli and sesame seeds and fry for 2–3 mins until golden and crisp. Remove from the heat.
To serve, divide the rice between bowls, spoon over the sausage & aubergine ragù and finish with a generous drizzle of the crispy chilli-ginger oil.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
